summary: Hotz defends his track record of avoiding big tech capture and explains his philosophy of building companies that resist centralized control. He argues that the future depends on who truly 'owns' the hardware and AI training infrastructure, not in a legal sense but in the hacker sense of having root access. Through comma.ai and tiny corp, he's pursuing a strategy of commoditizing compute and ensuring open source prevents rent-seeking pivots. He challenges readers to stop participating in systems that concentrate power, arguing that buying personal safety is both impossible and contemptible.
key_points: True ownership means having root access and control, not legal title - this will determine what the future looks like
NVIDIA's dominance comes from software lock-in, not hardware superiority, which is why commoditizing the petaflop matters
MIT licensing open source software is a defensive mechanism that prevents future pivots to rent-seeking even if company control is lost
Individual defection (selling out for personal safety) is a losing strategy that dooms everyone collectively
Sustainable profitability through simple business models (selling boxes for more than they cost) beats hypergrowth ponzi schemes
money_quotes: I think who owns the robots is going to be a key aspect of what the future looks like. And I don't mean 'owns' from a legalist perspective, I mean 'owns' as in the hacker meaning, like 'owning' the box. Who has root?
NVIDIA's value comes from their software, not their hardware, what else explains the huge gap between NVIDIA and AMD's market cap?
If you think you can somehow just buy safety for yourself, you are both wrong and pathetic.
The best hope any of us have is to maximize the number of things that survive.
If you and everyone else sell to bankers in hopes of buying a personal ticket out, we are all dead.
I'm not playing defect, and I shame you for doing so.
To everyone working on ads, surveillance, gambling, secret research, enshittification, cloud lock-in, what are you doing with your life?
It doesn't require everyone to stop, just enough people. And it starts with you.
key_insight: The future will be determined by who has true control over AI hardware and training infrastructure, making commoditization and open source critical strategies for preventing dystopian concentration of power.
